Now I have the full picture. Here's the diagnosis:

**Root cause**: iOS Safari (and some Android Chrome) doesn't reliably trigger a style recalculation for `dvh`/`dvw` after orientation change. The grid `width: min(90dvw, ...)` gets stale.

**Fix**: Use JS-driven CSS custom properties `--vw`/`--vh` (with `dvw`/`dvh` as no-JS fallbacks) so orientation change via `resize` forces reliable style recalculation.

Three file changes:
1. `clock.css` — swap `dvw`/`dvh` for `calc(var(--vw, 1dvw) * N)` pattern
2. `main.js` — add viewport updater, remove double-init of FaviconAnimator (it already self-inits at line 139 of favicon-animator.js)
3. `index.html` — load `main.js`

content.text: Let me verify the clock.css changes look right:

Good. Here's a summary of what was done:

**3 files changed:**

**`js/main.js`** — Rewritten as a proper entry point:
- Removed the duplicate `FaviconAnimator.init()` call (it already self-inits at the bottom of `favicon-animator.js`)
- Added `updateViewportUnits()` — sets `--vw` and `--vh` as CSS custom properties on `<html>` on load and on every `resize` event

**`styles/clock.css`** — Grid width expressions updated:
- `90dvw` → `calc(var(--vw, 1dvw) * 90)` (and same pattern for the other two breakpoints)
- The `dvw`/`dvh` fallbacks in `var(--vw, 1dvw)` mean no-JS still works

**`index.html`** — Added `<script src="js/main.js" defer></script>` before the other scripts

**Why this works**: On orientation change, iOS Safari fires `resize`. The listener updates `--vw`/`--vh` from `window.innerWidth`/`window.innerHeight` (which are always correct). Setting a CSS custom property on `documentElement` forces a style recalculation cascade, so the grid `width: min(...)` evaluates with fresh values. No changes to `clock.js` or the animation engine.

1→# Architecture — anthropicprinciple.ai
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→anthropicprinciple.ai is a kinetic clock art piece. The page presents 84 analogue mini-clocks arranged in a 6-row by 14-column grid; clock.js drives all hand positions through a continuous 30-second animation cycle that blends between four generative patterns before resolving to display the current time (or a countdown). clock-controls.html provides a settings panel that writes configuration to localStorage, which clock.js reads on load and re-reads on the storage event. A favicon animator runs on all pages, drawing a progressive square outline on a 32×32 canvas at approximately 10fps.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Page / Script Dependency Diagram
    10→
    11→```
    12→index.html
    13→├── styles/colors.css         (design tokens)
    14→├── styles/global.css         (base styles, flex centring)
    15→├── styles/clock.css          (clock tokens, grid, hand animations)
    16→├── styles/utilities.css      (u-* helper classes)
    17→├── styles/home.css           (aside, social links, hover animations)
    18→├── js/favicon-animator.js    (defer)
    19→└── js/clock.js               (defer)
    20→
    21→clock-controls.html
    22→├── styles/controls.css       (stylesheet)
    23→├── js/favicon-animator.js    (defer)
    24→└── js/controls.js            (defer) — form state, localStorage writes
    25→
    26→play.html
    27→├── styles/play.css           (stylesheet)
    28→└── (no scripts)
    29→```
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## CSS Architecture
    34→
    35→### Clock page
    36→
    37→index.html loads five stylesheets in order:
    38→
    39→1. `colors.css` — design tokens (loaded here so `--clk-bg` and global tokens are both available)
    40→2. `global.css` — body flex centring and base resets shared with other pages
    41→3. `clock.css` — self-contained clock tokens (`--clk-*`), the 6×14 grid (`.grid-14x6`), `.mc` cell containment, `.hand` compositor hints, and countdown-zero keyframes; body clock-specific overrides are scoped to `body.clock`
    42→4. `utilities.css` — `u-*` helper classes
    43→5. `home.css` — aside attribution, social link layout, Bluesky flutter animation, Claude/SoundCloud hover effects
    44→
    45→### Non-clock pages
    46→
    47→All other pages load stylesheets in the following order:
    48→
    49→1. `colors.css` — design tokens (must be first)
    50→2. `fonts.css` — font-family declarations
    51→3. `global.css` — reset, base elements, buttons, forms, 12-column grid
    52→4. `components.css` — header, logo, tooltip, alert, modal
    53→5. `[page].css` — page-specific overrides (e.g. `controls.css`, `play.css`)
    54→6. `utilities.css` — `u-*` helper classes (must be last to allow overrides)
    55→
    56→---
    57→
    58→## Design Token System
    59→
    60→`colors.css` is the single source of truth for all CSS custom properties. Light mode defaults are declared on `:root`. Dark mode overrides are applied via `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`, which can be further overridden by `[data-theme="light"]` or `[data-theme="dark"]` attributes on `<html>` for manual theme control.
    61→
    62→Font sizes use `em` units (all `--font-size-*` properties) so they scale relative to the element's inherited font size. Spacing uses `rem` units (all `--space-*` properties) so distances remain consistent regardless of local font-size inheritance. The browser default font size (16px) is never overridden on `html` or `body`.
    63→
    64→---
    65→
    66→## 12-Column Grid
    67→
    68→Defined in `global.css`. The pattern is `.container > .row > .col-*`. Mobile-first: all `.col-*` elements default to `grid-column: span 12` (full width, stacked). At 480px+, `.col-1` through `.col-4` span 6, and `.col-5` through `.col-8` span 8. At 768px+, every `.col-1` through `.col-12` spans its nominal number of columns. Container minimum width is 288px; maximum is 1440px. The clock page bypasses this grid entirely — it uses its own CSS grid declared in `clock.css`.
    69→
    70→---
    71→
    72→## Clock Engine (js/clock.js)
    73→
    74→### Grid structure
    75→
    76→84 cells are pre-rendered in HTML as `.mc` elements, each containing two `.hand` elements. clock.js references these via `document.querySelectorAll('#grid .hand')` and organises them into a flat `hands[]` array of `{h1, h2}` pairs indexed row-major. Hands pivot from a shared centre point (`transform-origin: bottom center`) and extend upward by half the cell diameter.
    77→
    78→### 30-second cycle
    79→
    80→The cycle is keyed to `Date.now()` modulo 30000ms and runs continuously. Phase boundaries (ms):
    81→
    82→| Window | Duration | Phase |
    83→|---|---|---|
    84→| 0 – 2000 | 2 s | Ease-out: time display to live pattern (60fps) |
    85→| 2000 – 23000 | 21 s | Blended patterns (throttled to ~30fps) |
    86→| 23000 – 25000 | 2 s | Ease-in: pattern to time display (60fps) |
    87→| 25000 – 30000 | 5 s | Static time display (throttled to 1fps) |
    88→
    89→### Four kinetic patterns
    90→
    91→`patternAngles()` computes base angle and spread for each of the 84 cells using the pattern index:
    92→
    93→| Index | Name | Computation |
    94→|---|---|---|
    95→| 0 | Radial wave | `atan2(dr, dc)` with time-phase rotation and fixed spread |
    96→| 1 | Linear sweep | Linear function of column, row, and time; zero spread |
    97→| 2 | Sinusoidal radial | `atan2(dr, dc)` with sinusoidal spread |
    98→| 3 | Distance-modulated spiral | `atan2` with `sqrt` distance field modulating phase and spread |
    99→
   100→Adjacent patterns crossfade over 1200ms using `blendedPatternAngles()`. The crossfade interpolates all 84 hand pairs using `lerpA()` (shortest-arc lerp) and an ease function.
   101→
   102→### Performance
   103→
   104→Five pre-allocated output buffers (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) are created at module initialisation as fixed-length arrays of 2-element sub-arrays. These are mutated in place on every frame — no per-frame heap allocations occur during the blend or ease windows.
   105→
   106→`apply()` holds a `Float64Array` of length 168 (`_lastAngles`) as a cache of the last value written to each hand. Before writing `style.transform`, it compares the rounded new angle against the cached value and skips the DOM write if they are equal, avoiding unnecessary style invalidations. Angles are rounded to 2 decimal places to reduce unique string allocations.
   107→
   108→Pattern-phase trig calculations (`atan2`, `sqrt`, `sin` per cell) are throttled to approximately 30fps (33ms minimum frame interval) because they are the most expensive per-frame work. The ease-in and ease-out windows run at full 60fps because they are short (2s each) and do not recompute pattern trig. The static display re-renders at most once per second.
   109→
   110→The rAF loop is started with `requestAnimationFrame(tick)`. A `visibilitychange` listener cancels the rAF when the tab is hidden and resumes it when the tab becomes visible again.
   111→
   112→---
   113→
   114→## Countdown Zero State
   115→
   116→When countdown mode reaches zero, `timeAngles()` sets `body.classList` to include `countdown-zero`. This class activates two CSS rules in `clock.css`:
   117→
   118→- `body.countdown-zero .hand` — applies `danger-pulse` keyframes: a 1.2s ease-in-out loop that alternates hand colour between `--clk-zero-hand` and `#ff5c50` with a 4-layer box-shadow glow.
   119→- `body.countdown-zero::after` — inserts a full-screen fixed pseudo-element with a radial gradient vignette that pulses in sync via `body-pulse` keyframes.
   120→
   121→---
   122→
   123→## Favicon Animator (js/favicon-animator.js)
   124→
   125→An IIFE module (`FaviconAnimator`) initialises on `DOMContentLoaded`. It creates an off-screen 32×32 canvas, sets up a linear gradient stroke style, and starts a rAF loop throttled to approximately 10fps (100ms minimum interval). Each frame draws a progressively longer square outline: top edge (0–25% of cycle), right edge (25–50%), bottom edge (50–75%), left edge (75–100%), then repeats. The completed frame is written to `favicon.href` via `canvas.toDataURL('image/png')`. A `visibilitychange` listener pauses the rAF when the tab is hidden and resumes it on visibility restore.
   126→
   127→---
   128→
   129→## Control Panel (clock-controls.html)
   130→
   131→The page loads `styles/controls.css`, which defines a fixed dark terminal theme using `--ctrl-*` custom properties. This theme is intentionally not system-preference responsive — the panel always renders dark. `js/controls.js` (deferred) handles all control panel behaviour: restoring saved values from localStorage on load, toggling section visibility via `.is-hidden` / `.is-active` classes when the mode radio changes, writing to localStorage on every input event, and computing a countdown end timestamp (`Date.now() + totalMs`) when the Start / Reset button is clicked.
   132→
   133→localStorage keys read by clock.js:
   134→
   135→| Key | Value |
   136→|---|---|
   137→| `clk_mode` | `"clock"` or `"countdown"` |
   138→| `clk_hours` | Integer UTC offset string (e.g. `"1"` for UTC+1) |
   139→| `clk_countdown_time` | Duration string in `"MM:SS"` format (e.g. `"05:00"`) |
   140→| `clk_countdown_end` | Unix timestamp in ms (absolute end time of countdown) |
   141→
   142→clock.js reads these on load via `loadConfig()` and re-reads them whenever another tab fires a `storage` event.
   143→
   144→---
   145→
   146→## Logger (js/logger.js)
   147→
   148→A development-only module. Entries are accumulated in a private `_buf` array in memory. No storage I/O occurs on individual `log()` calls. On `beforeunload`, `flush()` writes the full buffer to `sessionStorage` under the key `ap_dev_log`, and a summary object (count, URL, timestamp, entries) is written to `localStorage` under `ap_dev_log_summary`. The module self-tests localStorage and sessionStorage availability on initialisation and logs the results as buffered entries.
   149→
   150→Public API:
   151→
   152→| Method | Description |
   153→|---|---|
   154→| `log(section, message, data)` | Buffer a named entry with optional data payload |
   155→| `time(name)` | Start a named `performance.now()` timer |
   156→| `timeEnd(name)` | Stop timer; log elapsed ms |
   157→| `flush()` | Write buffer to sessionStorage immediately |
   158→| `dump()` | Print all entries to the browser console |
   159→| `clear()` | Empty buffer and remove sessionStorage entry |
   160→

1→# Kinetic Grid Clock — Reference
     2→
     3→**Project**: erudit0rum — 84 mini-clocks (6 rows × 14 columns), inspired by Humans Since 1982 *A Million Times*.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Architecture
     8→
     9→### Grid layout (14 cols)
    10→```
    11→[digit H-tens 3 cols][digit H-units 3 cols][colon 2 cols][digit M-tens 3 cols][digit M-units 3 cols]
    12→```
    13→
    14→### 30s cycle
    15→| Window | Duration | Phase |
    16→|--------|----------|-------|
    17→| 0 → 2s | 2s | Reverse ease: time display → pattern |
    18→| 2s → 23s | 21s | 4 blended patterns |
    19→| 23s → 25s | 2s | Forward ease: pattern → time display |
    20→| 25s → 30s | 5s | Static time display |
    21→
    22→Between patterns, hands crossfade over a 1.2s blend window. Both ease transitions use an ease-in-out curve (`x < 0.5 ? 2x² : 1−(−2x+2)²/2`).
    23→
    24→### Modes
    25→- **clock** — shows `HH:MM` (UTC + configurable offset). Default mode.
    26→- **countdown** — shows `MM:SS` counting down from a stored end timestamp. Freezes on `00:00` with a pulsating red glow ("danger time") until reset via controls.
    27→
    28→Settings persisted in `localStorage`: `clk_mode`, `clk_hours`, `clk_countdown_time`, `clk_countdown_end`.
    29→
    30→### CSS colour tokens (`styles/clock.css`)
    31→| Variable | Default | Purpose |
    32→|----------|---------|---------|
    33→| `--clk-bg` | `#050505` | Page background |
    34→| `--clk-face` | `#0e0e0e` | Clock face fill |
    35→| `--clk-border` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.04)` | Clock circle border |
    36→| `--clk-hand` | `#4f001e` | Hand colour |
    37→| `--clk-zero-hand` | `#ff2020` | Hand colour at countdown zero |
    38→| `--clk-zero-glow` | `rgba(255,32,32,0.45)` | Glow at countdown zero |
    39→
    40→### Patterns (4 choreographies in `js/clock.js`)
    41→| Index | Style |
    42→|-------|-------|
    43→| 0 | Concentric ripple (elliptical atan2, rotating) |
    44→| 1 | Diagonal wave (linear phase across grid) |
    45→| 2 | Radial V (circular atan2, spread oscillates) |
    46→| 3 | Vortex (radial distance + rotation, spread oscillates) |
    47→
    48→---
    49→
    50→## Digit Reference
    51→
    52→Each digit occupies a **3-column × 6-row** sub-grid of analog clock faces.
    53→Position notation: `H:M` where hour hand = H×30° and minute hand = M×6°.
    54→
    55→## Angle key
    56→
    57→| Notation  | Hands     | Shape | JS const |
    58→|-----------|-----------|-------|----------|
    59→| `3:45`    | →←        | ─     | `H`      |
    60→| `12:30`   | ↑↓        | │     | `V`      |
    61→| `6:15`    | ↓→        | ┌     | `TL`     |
    62→| `9:30`    | ↓←        | ┐     | `TR`     |
    63→| `12:15`   | ↑→        | └     | `BL`     |
    64→| `12:45`   | ↑←        | ┘     | `BR`     |
    65→| `6:30`    | ↓↓        | ·̣     | `I_D` (interior, both down) |
    66→| `12:00`   | ↑↑        | ·     | `I_U` (interior, both up)   |
    67→| `1:7.5`   | ↗↗        | ╱     | `NE`     |
    68→| `10:52.5` | ↖↖        | ╲     | `NW`     |
    69→| `4:22.5`  | ↘↘        | ╲     | `SE`     |
    70→| `7:37.5`  | ↙↙        | ╱     | `SW`     |
    71→
    72→---
    73→

    77→┌ ─ ┐   TL,  H,   TR

    80→│ │ │   V,   V,   V
    81→│ · │   V,   I_U, V

1→# Refactoring Summary — anthropicprinciple.ai
     2→
     3→## Project at a Glance
     4→
     5→anthropicprinciple.ai is a browser-based kinetic clock art piece. 84 analogue mini-clocks are arranged in a 6×14 grid; their hands are driven by a JavaScript rAF loop that cycles through generative animation patterns before periodically resolving to display the current time or a running countdown. The project has three HTML pages: `index.html` (the clock), `clock-controls.html` (the settings panel), and `play.html` (additional page). The technology stack is vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — no build tools, no frameworks, no external dependencies.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## CSS Layer Summary
    10→
    11→| File | Scope | Loads on |
    12→|---|---|---|
    13→| clock.css | Clock tokens, grid, hand animation, keyframes | index.html |
    14→| colors.css | Design tokens: light/dark mode, all custom properties | Non-clock pages |
    15→| fonts.css | System font-family stacks | Non-clock pages |
    16→| global.css | Base elements, forms, 12-column grid | Non-clock pages |
    17→| components.css | Header, logo, tooltip, alert, modal | Non-clock pages |
    18→| controls.css | Clock-controls panel (fixed dark theme) | clock-controls.html |
    19→| banner.css | Banner section with fluid typography | As needed |
    20→| border-effect.css | Animated conic-gradient border component | As needed |
    21→| utilities.css | `u-*` helper classes | All non-clock pages |
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## JS Summary
    26→
    27→| File | Purpose | Loaded on |
    28→|---|---|---|
    29→| clock.js | Clock engine — rAF loop, pattern generation, angle application | index.html |
    30→| controls.js | Controls panel — localStorage read/write, section visibility toggling | clock-controls.html |
    31→| favicon-animator.js | Animated canvas favicon at ~10fps | All pages |
    32→| logger.js | Dev logger — in-memory buffer, flush on unload | Opt-in (not loaded by default) |
    33→| main.js | Reserved entry point | Not currently loaded |
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Performance Profile
    38→
    39→**Zero external fonts, zero third-party scripts on the clock page.** The entire clock experience is delivered with 1 CSS file and 2 JavaScript files.
    40→
    41→**GPU compositing**: `will-change: transform` on all 168 `.hand` elements promotes each to its own compositor layer. Transform updates written by clock.js are applied by the GPU thread, not the main thread.
    42→
    43→**Style isolation**: `contain: layout style paint` on each `.mc` cell scopes style recalculation to that cell. A transform write on any one hand cannot invalidate the style tree for the rest of the document.
    44→
    45→**rAF management**: the loop pauses on `visibilitychange` when the tab is hidden (both clock.js and favicon-animator.js). No CPU or GPU work occurs in backgrounded tabs.
    46→
    47→**Frame rate strategy**:
    48→- Pattern trig phase (21s window): ~30fps throttle — `atan2`, `sqrt`, and `sin` per cell is the most expensive computation
    49→- Ease-in / ease-out phases (2s each): 60fps — no pattern trig, only lerp operations on pre-computed snapshots
    50→- Static time display (5s window): 1fps — re-renders only when the wall-clock second changes
    51→
    52→**No per-frame heap allocations**: all angle output arrays (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) are pre-allocated at module initialisation and mutated in place. The `Float64Array` angle cache (`_lastAngles`) in `apply()` further eliminates DOM writes for values that have not changed.
    53→
    54→**Images**: where used, `srcset` with 250w and 500w resolution variants delivers appropriately sized images to each device. `loading="lazy"` defers off-screen image decoding.
    55→
    56→---
    57→
    58→## Key Constraints to Preserve
    59→
    60→The following constraints must not be violated when modifying any file in the project:
    61→
    62→- `clock.css` and `clock.js` may only be modified for performance reasons. Visual behaviour, timing values (cycle duration, phase boundaries, ease durations), and pattern mathematics must not change.
    63→- No inline styles anywhere. All visual state must be expressed through CSS class toggling (`.is-hidden`, `.is-active`, `.countdown-zero`).
    64→- No `max-width` media queries. All responsive breakpoints are `min-width` only (mobile-first).
    65→- No visited state in any CSS file.
    66→- All `font-size` values must use `em` units. All distance and spacing values (padding, margin, gap, width) must use `rem` units.
    67→- Every colour, spacing value, transition duration, and font family must be expressed as a CSS custom property. No bare hex values or raw pixel measurements in component or page stylesheets.
    68→

## Files Read (Complete Contents)

### JavaScript Files

**`js/controls.js`** (77 lines)

- Computes countdown end timestamp on Start/Reset click
- Storage keys: `clk_mode`, `clk_hours`, `clk_countdown_time`, `clk_countdown_end`

**`js/logger.js`** (160 lines)
- Development logger with in-memory buffering
- Flushed to sessionStorage only on beforeunload (no sync I/O on log calls)
- Provides: `Logger.log()`, `Logger.time()`, `Logger.timeEnd()`, `Logger.dump()`, `Logger.clear()`, `Logger.flush()`
- Self-test on init: checks localStorage/sessionStorage availability and logs navigation timing
- Entirely separate from application code—can be included or excluded without affecting clock/UI

### CSS Files

**`styles/components.css`** (181 lines)
- Reusable UI components: header, logo, main layout, tooltip, alert, modal
- Mobile-first with min-width breakpoints
- Header: flex, bordered, responsive padding and logo sizing
- Tooltip: data-tip attribute, positioned absolutely, appears on hover/focus
- Alert: with `.mod-info`, `.mod-success`, `.mod-warning`, `.mod-danger` variants
- Modal: backdrop with dialog, close button, title, body with fixed z-index (200)

**`styles/controls.css`** (237 lines)
- Fixed dark terminal theme for clock-controls.html (intentionally not system-preference responsive)
- Custom tokens: `--ctrl-bg`, `--ctrl-bg-panel`, `--ctrl-bg-section`, `--ctrl-text`, etc.
- Components: `.panel`, `.panel-nav`, `.ctrl-section`, `.ctrl-radio-label`, `.ctrl-field`, `.ctrl-btn`, `.ctrl-status`
- Courier New font, 0.875em base, radio inputs with 14px custom accent color
- Reduced-motion support for transitions
- `.is-hidden` state class for toggling section visibility

**`styles/banner.css`** (63 lines)
- Full-width gradient banner with fluid heading typography
- Mobile-first responsive banner sizing (200px → 250px → 300px height)
- `.banner-title`: fluid typography using `clamp()` (1.4em → 2.4em at mobile, up to 4em at 768px+)
- White text on dark gradient, forced white for contrast
- Supports `prefers-contrast: more` and `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`

**`styles/border-effect.css`** (125 lines)
- Animated conic-gradient rotating border effect
- Structure: `.border-effect-wrap` > `.border-effect` (glow/blur layer) + `.border-effect-btn` (button above)
- Uses `::before` for rotating conic-gradient, `::after` for inner fill (cutout)
- Animation: `border-effect-rotate` at `var(--animation-duration)` (linear infinite)
- Responsive max sizing: 120px × 140px (mobile) → 150px × 180px (480px) → 200px × 250px (768px)
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`

content.text:
Gemini Response:

This code review identifies violations of the project's `CLAUDE.md` conventions and general web best practices.

### 1. Convention Violations (High Priority)

*   **`banner.css`**: Contains `!important`. This is a direct violation of the "No !important in CSS" rule.
*   **`home.css` & `clock.css`**: Hardcoded hex codes (e.g., `#292b31`, `#727786`, `#050505`, `#FF1B0E`). All colors must use CSS custom properties from `colors.css`.
*   **`clock.css`**: Uses a `max-height` media query. The convention specifies "Mobile-first min-width media queries only."
*   **`play.css`**: Includes a redundant `box-sizing: border-box` reset. This is already handled in `global.css`.
*   **`main.js`**: Uses `var`. Modern vanilla JS should use `const` and `let` to prevent hoisting issues and improve readability.

### 2. Performance & Browser Memory

*   **`main.js` (Viewport Hack)**: The `resize` event listener fires continuously during window resizing, causing layout thrashing.
    *   *Action*: Use `requestAnimationFrame` or a debounce function.
    *   *Better Action*: Since you are already using `dvw` and `dvh` units in `clock.css`, the JS-based `--vw`/`--vh` calculation is likely redundant in modern browsers. Remove `main.js` if support for very old browsers isn't required.
*   **`clock.css` (`will-change`)**: You have 84 clocks, each with 2 hands, totaling 168 elements with `will-change: transform`.
    *   *Risk*: This creates 168 separate compositor layers, which can lead to high GPU memory consumption and "layer exploding," potentially crashing mobile browsers.
    *   *Action*: Remove `will-change: transform` from `.hand`. Modern browsers optimize `transform` animations automatically. Only re-add it if you observe actual stuttering.
*   **`controls.js`**: Writes to `localStorage` on every `input` event.
    *   *Action*: Debounce the storage write (e.g., 250ms) to prevent excessive disk I/O, especially on mobile devices.

### 3. Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)

*   **`home.css` (Contrast)**: The color `#727786` on background `#292b31` (Accreditation side text) has a contrast ratio of **2.85:1**.
    *   *Action*: This fails the WCAG AA requirement of **4.5:1**. Increase the brightness of the text color.
*   **`index.html` (Clock Grid)**: The 84 `.mc` divs are empty and likely lack semantic meaning to screen readers.
    *   *Action*: Ensure the `main[aria-label="Kinetic clock"]` or the grid itself has an `aria-live="polite"` attribute if the clock hands convey time-sensitive information, or ensure the current time is available as text for screen readers (e.g., a `.u-sr-only` clock readout).

### 4. Code Organisation & Clean Code

*   **`critical.css`**: This is a "legacy placeholder" not loaded by any HTML.
    *   *Action*: Delete the file. Dead code increases maintenance overhead.
*   **`clock-controls.html`**: Does not load `main.js`.
    *   *Issue*: If `main.js` is intended to provide global utilities or viewport fixes, the controls page will behave inconsistently compared to the index.
*   **`controls.css`**: Defines its own `--ctrl-*` tokens.
    *   *Action*: Move these to `colors.css` under a specific section to maintain a single source of truth for the design system.

### 5. Browser Security

*   **`controls.js` (Input Validation)**: The script reads from `localStorage` and applies values directly.
    *   *Action*: Sanitize and validate the types of values retrieved from `localStorage` before using them in logic or DOM manipulation to prevent issues if the storage is tampered with.

### 6. CSS Specificity & Layout

*   **`home.css` (`aside.side`)**: The use of `position: absolute` with `right: calc(-50vw + 1.25rem)` and a `-90deg` rotation is highly fragile.
    *   *Issue*: This will likely cause horizontal overflow (scrollbars) on various screen widths or break when the viewport is narrow.
    *   *Action*: Use `writing-mode: vertical-rl` or `transform-origin` adjustments to place vertical text more reliably without "guessing" the negative offset with `vw`. Ensure `overflow-x: hidden` is set on the body if this design is required.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/mat/.claude/skills/git-commit-messaging

# Git Commit Messaging

Generate commit messages that conform to the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) specification. These messages are machine-readable, human-friendly, and form the backbone of semantic versioning and automated changelogs.

---

## Message Structure

```
<type>[optional scope]: <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]
```

Every part serves a purpose. The type + description line is the minimum viable commit. Body and footers add depth when changes are complex or have downstream implications.

---

## Types

| Type | When to use |
|------|-------------|
| `feat` | New feature or capability added to the codebase |
| `fix` | Bug fix — patches broken behaviour |
| `docs` | Documentation only (README, comments, JSDoc) |
| `style` | Formatting, whitespace, semicolons — no logic change |
| `refactor` | Code restructured without adding features or fixing bugs |
| `perf` | Performance improvement |
| `test` | Adding or correcting tests |
| `build` | Build system, dependencies, package.json, webpack, etc. |
| `ci` | CI/CD configuration (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, etc.) |
| `chore` | Maintenance tasks that don't affect src or test files |
| `revert` | Reverts a previous commit |

When in doubt between two types, pick the one that best describes the *primary intent* of the change.

---

## Scope (optional)

A noun in parentheses describing which part of the codebase was changed:

```
feat(auth): add OAuth2 login flow
fix(cart): prevent negative quantity values
refactor(api): extract rate-limiting middleware
```

Use scope when it meaningfully narrows context. Omit it when the change is broad or the repo is small. Scope should match the team's existing conventions if any exist — check recent commits for patterns.

---

## Description

- Immediately follows the `: ` after type/scope
- Imperative mood, present tense: `add`, `fix`, `update` — not `added`, `fixes`, `updating`
- No capital letter at the start
- No full stop at the end
- Under 72 characters ideally; definitely under 100

Good: `fix(parser): handle empty array edge case`
Bad: `Fixed the parser to handle arrays that are empty.`

---

## Body (optional)

Include a body when the *why* or *how* isn't obvious from the description alone. Separate from the description with one blank line.

```
feat(checkout): add address validation on submit

Previously, invalid addresses could reach the payment step, causing
silent failures in the Stripe webhook. Validation now runs client-side
on form submit and server-side before charge creation.
```

The body is free-form. Write as many paragraphs as needed. Focus on motivation and context, not a line-by-line description of code (the diff does that).

---

## Footers (optional)

Footers follow the body (or description, if no body), separated by a blank line. Each footer is a token, a separator, and a value:

```
Reviewed-by: Alice <alice@example.com>
Refs: #142
BREAKING CHANGE: the `parseConfig` function now requires an options object
```

**Breaking changes** must be flagged one of two ways:

1. Footer: `BREAKING CHANGE: <description of what changed and migration path>`
2. `!` in the type/scope prefix: `feat(config)!: require options object in parseConfig`

Both are valid. You can use both together. If using `!`, you may omit the footer, but the footer gives consumers more migration detail.

---

## Worked examples

**Simple bugfix:**
```
fix(nav): correct active link highlight on mobile
```

**New feature with scope:**
```
feat(search): add debounced autocomplete to product search
```

**Refactor with body:**
```
refactor(db): replace raw queries with query builder

Direct SQL strings were scattered across three service files, making
schema changes risky. Centralised through Knex query builder to
improve testability and allow easier schema migrations.
```

**Breaking change with footer:**
```
feat(api)!: remove v1 endpoints

BREAKING CHANGE: /api/v1/* routes have been removed. Migrate to /api/v2/*.
See migration guide at docs/v2-migration.md.
```

**Dependency update:**
```
build(deps): upgrade Tailwind to v4

Refs: #201
```

**Multi-footer commit:**
```
fix(auth): prevent session hijack on logout

Clear all active session tokens on logout rather than just the
current one, to invalidate concurrent sessions.

Reviewed-by: Mat Havelock <mat@example.com>
Fixes: #87
```

---

## Working from staged changes or a diff

When the user asks you to generate a commit message from their current changes:

1. Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing is staged) to read the actual diff
2. Identify the primary intent — what is fundamentally changing?
3. Identify the scope — which module, component, or file area is the focus?
4. Check for breaking changes — are any public APIs, interfaces, or contracts altered?
5. Draft the message and show it to the user before running `git commit`

If the diff spans multiple unrelated concerns, flag this. A commit should ideally represent one logical change. You can suggest splitting if appropriate, but don't be pedantic about it — real projects often have mixed commits.

---

## Working from a description

When the user describes what they did in plain English:

- Extract the type from what was accomplished
- Extract the scope from what area was affected
- Rewrite the description in imperative form
- Ask if there's a body-worthy reason for the change (only if it's non-obvious)
- Propose the message and confirm before committing

---

## Checking existing conventions

Before generating a message, if the repo has a commit history, it's worth a quick glance:

```bash
git log --oneline -10
```

Match existing scope naming conventions if they're consistent. If the project already uses `feat(ui):` don't introduce `feat(frontend):` without reason.

---

## Output format

Always present the commit message in a code block so it's easy to copy:

```
feat(auth): add JWT refresh token rotation

Refresh tokens are now single-use and rotated on each request.
Previous tokens are invalidated immediately, reducing the window
for token theft.

BREAKING CHANGE: clients must handle 401 responses and re-authenticate
when a refresh token is rejected.
```

Then offer to run the commit directly if the user wants, using:

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
<message here>
EOF
)"
```

Use the heredoc form to preserve multiline messages correctly.

# Improvements Log — anthropicprinciple.ai Technical improvements made to the project. Each section describes what changed and the reasoning. --- ## Standards and CSS Architecture **CLAUDE.md** rewritten as a comprehensive project reference covering brand guidelines, CSS architecture, JS module descriptions, performance constraints, and naming conventions. **CSS brand tokens updated** throughout `colors.css`: Arial and Verdana are declared as system font stacks (`--font-family-body`, `--font-family-heading`) with no external font requests. The brand palette (`#1d1f24` / `#a1b1ca`) is expressed as CSS custom properties used consistently across all button, text, border, and input token groups. **Typography units standardised**: all `--font-size-*` values use `em` so they scale with inherited context. All `--space-*` values use `rem` for layout distances that remain consistent regardless of local font-size changes. The `html` element font-size is never overridden — the browser default of 16px is preserved for user accessibility. **Mobile-first refactor** applied across all stylesheets. Base styles target the smallest screen. All responsive breakpoints use `min-width` only — no `max-width` breakpoints exist anywhere in the codebase. **Link colour** inherits from text colour (`color: inherit`) in both light and dark mode, so links read as body text unless explicitly styled. Hover and focus-visible states are defined; no visited state is set anywhere. **Component naming convention** applied throughout: `.component-element` structure for component parts, `.mod-*` for variant modifiers, `.is-*` for toggled state classes, `.u-*` prefix for utility classes. **Hover, focus-visible, and active states** defined on all interactive elements. No visited state. Focus rings use `outline: 2px solid var(--color-focus)` with `outline-offset: 2px`. **controls.css extracted** from inline styles into an external stylesheet. A dedicated `--ctrl-*` token namespace isolates the panel's fixed dark theme from the global design token system. No `--color-*` tokens are used on the controls page. **Tooltip, alert, and modal components** added to `components.css`. ARIA usage patterns documented in CSS comments: tooltip visible on `:focus-visible` for keyboard users; alert uses `role="alert"`; modal requires `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-labelledby`, focus trapping, Escape-key close, and backdrop-click close. **Orientation landscape media query** added in `global.css` and `clock.css` to handle small devices rotated to landscape, reducing vertical padding to recover viewport height. --- ## HTML **index.html** updated with: semantic `
` wrapper, Open Graph meta tags (`og:type`, `og:url`, `og:title`, `og:description`), canonical ``, `theme-color` meta, and a JSON-LD `WebApplication` schema block. All favicons and web manifest references are present. **clock-controls.html** updated with: all inline styles removed and replaced with `controls.css` classes; proper `

---
name: anthropicprinciple.ai project state
description: Current architecture, file structure, CSS load order, JS modules, and key constraints for anthropicprinciple.ai
type: project
---

## What the site is

Kinetic clock art piece. 84 analogue mini-clocks (6 rows × 14 cols) animate through generative patterns then resolve to display current time or a countdown. Three pages: index.html (clock), clock-controls.html (settings), play.html (SoundCloud playlists).

Stack: vanilla HTML + CSS + JS. No build tools, no frameworks, no external dependencies.

## CSS load order

### index.html
1. styles/colors.css — design tokens
2. styles/global.css — body flex centring, base resets
3. styles/clock.css — clock tokens, .grid-14x6, .mc, .hand, countdown-zero keyframes (body.clock scoped)
4. styles/utilities.css — u-* helpers
5. styles/home.css — aside, social links, Bluesky flutter, Claude/SoundCloud hover effects

### clock-controls.html
- styles/controls.css only (self-contained dark theme, --ctrl-* tokens)

## JS files

| File | Purpose | Loaded on |
|---|---|---|
| js/clock.js | rAF loop, pattern engine, angle application | index.html |
| js/controls.js | localStorage read/write, section toggle | clock-controls.html |
| js/favicon-animator.js | Canvas favicon ~10fps | All pages |
| js/logger.js | Dev logger — opt-in, not loaded by default | — |

## Key constraints (must not be violated)

- `.grid-14x6` class on the grid div; `id="grid"` is the JS hook — never remove the id
- `body.clock` on index.html body — clock-specific body overrides are scoped to this class
- No inline styles anywhere
- No max-width media queries — mobile-first min-width only
- No :visited state
- font-size: em units; spacing/layout: rem units
- All colours via CSS custom properties — no bare hex in component CSS
- clock.css and clock.js: do not change visual behaviour, timing, or pattern maths

## Performance notes

- 168 .hand elements: will-change:transform promotes each to GPU compositor layer
- .mc: contain:layout style paint scopes recalc to each cell
- rAF loops pause on visibilitychange (both clock.js and favicon-animator.js)
- Pre-allocated angle buffers — zero per-frame heap allocations
- Float64Array cache skips DOM writes when angle unchanged
- Pattern trig ~30fps throttled; ease phases 60fps; static display 1fps

## Social icons (index.html)

- Bluesky: inline SVG butterfly (class=bsky-flutter), flutter animation in home.css
- SoundCloud: assets/icons/soundcloud.svg, class=links-soundcloud, ripple animation in home.css
- Claude credit: assets/icons/claude-ai-icon.svg, class=credit-claude, spin animation in home.css

## Loader

- `.loader` div with mini clock face rendered before rAF starts
- Dismissed by clock.js using a double-rAF after first paint (ensures grid is visible before removing loader)

## Favicon animator

- `init()` removes all static favicon `<link>` elements and injects a fresh canvas-backed one on startup
- Previously, static ICO/SVG link elements overrode canvas updates — this fix is required for animated favicon to work

## JS extraction / hygiene

- `controls.js` extracted from inline `<script>` in clock-controls.html — logic now lives in `js/controls.js`
- All `target="_blank"` links carry `rel="noopener noreferrer"`

## Meta / SEO (index.html)

- Meta description improved
- `og:image` and `og:description` updated for better social sharing

## Recent work (session 2026-03-15)

- body.clock class on index.html — clock-specific body styles scoped here
- .grid renamed to .grid-14x6 in clock.css + index.html (id="grid" unchanged)
- Social icons added to index.html:
  - Bluesky: inline SVG butterfly (class=bsky-flutter), CSS flutter animation
  - SoundCloud: assets/icons/soundcloud.svg with ripple animation (class=links-soundcloud)
  - Claude credit: assets/icons/claude-ai-icon.svg with anticlockwise-dip-then-spin (class=credit-claude)
- Loader: .loader div with mini clock face, dismissed by clock.js double-rAF after first paint
- Favicon animator fix: init() now removes all static favicon link elements and injects a fresh one — previously static ICO/SVG overrode canvas updates
- controls.js extracted from clock-controls.html inline script
- All target="_blank" links have rel="noopener noreferrer"
- Meta description, og:image, og:description improved on index.html

## Git state

Branch: main. All changes committed and pushed. Site live at https://anthropicprinciple.ai/

---
name: Code standards enforced on anthropicprinciple.ai
description: Coding conventions, naming rules, and review standards enforced on this project — apply these on every task
type: feedback
---

## CSS

- Mobile-first: base styles for smallest screen, min-width media queries only — never max-width
- Properties alphabetised within every rule block
- No !important anywhere
- No inline styles in HTML
- Component naming: .component-element, .mod-variant, .is-state, .u-utility, .js-hook
- em for font-size, rem for spacing/layout dimensions
- All colours as CSS custom properties — no bare hex or rgb() in component CSS
- No :visited state in any file
- Dark mode via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) in colors.css
- prefers-reduced-motion blocks required alongside any new animation

## JavaScript

- IIFEs or module pattern — no globals
- const/let only (no var)
- Single quotes for strings
- Semicolons always
- camelCase variables/functions, TitleCase classes, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE constants
- Event listeners via addEventListener — no inline handlers in HTML
- No innerHTML, no eval()
- All scripts loaded with defer attribute

## HTML

- No inline styles
- No inline event handlers
- All target="_blank" links: rel="noopener noreferrer" (both words required)
- All images: alt, width, height attributes
- Semantic elements: main, aside, nav, section, article — not divs
- Each page: lang="en" on <html>, theme-color meta, canonical link

## Git

- Conventional Commits format: type(scope): description
- Check in after each logical task
- Show git diff before committing

## Workflow

- Stop and show git diff between each task
- Update docs (ARCHITECTURE.md, IMPROVEMENTS.md, REFACTORING_SUMMARY.md) when structure changes
- Update memory when project state changes significantly

content.content: 1→# File Manifest — anthropicprinciple.ai 2→ 3→## Core HTML 4→ 5→**index.html** — Full-screen kinetic clock. Loads `styles/clock.css` as the sole stylesheet. Scripts `js/favicon-animator.js` and `js/clock.js` are both loaded with `defer`. Contains the pre-rendered 84 `.mc` cells (6 rows × 14 columns), Open Graph meta, canonical link, theme-color meta, and a JSON-LD `WebApplication` schema block. 6→ 7→**clock-controls.html** — Settings panel for configuring clock mode and countdown. Loads `styles/controls.css`. An inline IIFE script handles form state restoration from localStorage, section visibility toggling via `.is-hidden` / `.is-active` classes, localStorage writes on every input event, and countdown end-time calculation on button click. The status element uses `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"`. `robots` meta is set to `noindex`. 8→ 9→**play.html** — Additional page. 10→ 11→--- 12→ 13→## Styles 14→ 15→**clock.css** — Self-contained stylesheet loaded exclusively by index.html. Includes its own `*` reset, all `--clk-*` design tokens (background, face, border, hand colour, zero glow), the 6×14 `display: grid` layout with fluid `min()` width, `.mc` cell with `contain: layout style paint`, `.hand` elements with `will-change: transform` and `transform-origin: bottom center`, and the `danger-pulse` / `body-pulse` keyframes for countdown-zero state. Also contains landscape orientation media query for small-screen layout. 16→ 17→**colors.css** — Design token source of truth for all non-clock pages. `:root` declares light mode defaults: surface colours (`--color-bg`, `--color-bg-alt`, `--color-bg-raised`), text colours, border colours, primary and secondary button colours, link colours, form input colours, focus ring colour, and accent colours. Font sizes use `em` (`--font-size-h1` through `--font-size-xs`). Spacing uses `rem` (`--space-xs` through `--space-3xl`). ## Code Review — 2026-03-20 (Iteration 1)

Full review against CLAUDE.md conventions, ARCHITECTURE.md, and best-practices docs. Gemini MCP consulted for independent second opinion.

**Deleted**
- `styles/critical.css` — legacy placeholder, never loaded by any HTML page. Dead code confirmed.

**JS — Clean Code**
- `main.js`: `var` → `const/let` (CLAUDE.md convention). rAF throttle added to `resize` listener (`rafPending` flag) — prevents layout thrashing during continuous scroll/resize on mobile. Removed stale `DOMContentLoaded` wrapper (redundant with `defer`). Removed duplicate `FaviconAnimator.init()` call (favicon-animator.js already self-inits at load time).
- `main.js` added to `clock-controls.html` — viewport orientation fix now applies consistently on both pages.

**JS — Security / Robustness**
- `controls.js`: localStorage values are now sanitized before use. `clk_hours` is parsed as integer and clamped to valid range (-12–14); `clk_countdown_time` is validated against `/^\d{1,2}:\d{2}$/` regex before applying to the input. Prevents stale or corrupted localStorage from breaking the UI.
- `controls.js`: debounce helper added (250ms) for `hours` and `countdown-time` inputs — prevents excessive localStorage writes on rapid keystrokes or arrow-key stepping.

**CSS — Convention Compliance**
- `home.css`: hardcoded hex palette (`#292b31`, `#727786`, `#5c6b94`, `#4d5f8c`) extracted to `--side-*` CSS custom properties at the top of the file. Satisfies the "all colours via CSS custom properties" rule.
- `home.css`: double-semicolon typo (`padding: 0.25rem 0.66rem;;`) fixed.
- `banner.css`: hardcoded `#ffffff` on `.banner-title` extracted to `--banner-title-color` CSS custom property.
- `clock.css`: explanatory comment added for `--vw`/`--vh` orientation-change fix.

**Items reviewed and left unchanged**
- `will-change: transform` on `.hand` — deliberate GPU compositor optimisation per CLAUDE.md performance notes. Not removed.
- `max-height: 500px` orientation query in `clock.css` — CLAUDE.md prohibits `max-width`, not `max-height`. Valid use.
- `--ctrl-*` tokens in `controls.css` — intentionally isolated from global token system (controls page is self-contained dark theme).
- `play.css` box-sizing reset — needed; play.html does not load global.css.
- `home.css` aside font-family (Gill Sans) — distinct design element, intentional deviation.

**Remaining issues (not fixed — see iteration 2 list)**
- WCAG contrast: `.side p` text `#727786` on background `#292b31` = ~2.85:1, fails AA (4.5:1 required).
- `home.css` aside positioning: `right: calc(-50vw + 1.25rem)` and `top: -17rem` are magic-number values fragile to viewport changes.
- `home.css` `.side p` font-family (Gill Sans) deviates from project's two-font system (Verdana/Arial).

---

## Code Review — 2026-03-15

All `--space-*` values use `rem` for layout distances that remain consistent regardless of local font-size changes. The `html` element font-size is never overridden — the browser default of 16px is preserved for user accessibility. **Mobile-first refactor** applied across all stylesheets. Base styles target the smallest screen. All responsive breakpoints use `min-width` only — no `max-width` breakpoints exist anywhere in the codebase. **Link colour** inherits from text colour (`color: inherit`) in both light and dark mode, so links read as body text unless explicitly styled. toolUseResult.originalFile: # File Manifest — anthropicprinciple.ai ## Core HTML **index.html** — Full-screen kinetic clock. Loads `styles/clock.css` as the sole stylesheet. Scripts `js/favicon-animator.js` and `js/clock.js` are both loaded with `defer`. Contains the pre-rendered 84 `.mc` cells (6 rows × 14 columns), Open Graph meta, canonical link, theme-color meta, and a JSON-LD `WebApplication` schema block. **clock-controls.html** — Settings panel for configuring clock mode and countdown. Loads `styles/controls.css`. An inline IIFE script handles form state restoration from localStorage, section visibility toggling via `.is-hidden` / `.is-active` classes, localStorage writes on every input event, and countdown end-time calculation on button click. The status element uses `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"`. `robots` meta is set to `noindex`. **play.html** — Additional page. --- ## Styles **clock.css** — Self-contained stylesheet loaded exclusively by index.html. Includes its own `*` reset, all `--clk-*` design tokens (background, face, border, hand colour, zero glow), the 6×14 `display: grid` layout with fluid `min()` width, `.mc` cell with `contain: layout style paint`, `.hand` elements with `will-change: transform` and `transform-origin: bottom center`, and the `danger-pulse` / `body-pulse` keyframes for countdown-zero state. Also contains landscape orientation media query for small-screen layout. **colors.css** — Design token source of truth for all non-clock pages. `:root` declares light mode defaults: surface colours (`--color-bg`, `--color-bg-alt`, `--color-bg-raised`), text colours, border colours, primary and secondary button colours, link colours, form input colours, focus ring colour, and accent colours. Font sizes use `em` (`--font-size-h1` through `--font-size-xs`). Spacing uses `rem` (`--space-xs` through `--space-3xl`). Also declares `--font-family-*`, `--border-*`, `--transition-*`, and `--ease-*` tokens. Dark mode overrides applied via `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`. Manual overrides available via `[data-theme="light"]` and `[data-theme="dark"]` on ``. **fonts.css** — Font-family declarations only. Verdana (headings), Arial (body), Courier New (mono). All system fonts — no external font requests are made. **global.css** — Box-model reset, base element defaults (`html`, `body`, `h1`–`h3`, `p`, `img`, `video`, `canvas`), link styles (`color: inherit`; hover and focus-visible states; no visited state), base `button` reset, `.btn` / `.btn-primary` / `.btn-secondary` component classes with 44px minimum touch targets, full form element styles for text inputs, textarea, select, and custom-styled checkbox and radio inputs using `appearance: none`. 12-column grid system (`.container`, `.row`, `.col-1` through `.col-12`) — mobile-first with `min-width` breakpoints at 480px and 768px. Orientation landscape media query for small devices. `prefers-reduced-motion` block that collapses all animation and transition durations. **components.css** — Header (flex, border-bottom, focus-visible ring), `.logo` with responsive height breakpoints, `main` layout defaults for non-clock pages. Tooltip: `.tooltip` with `data-tip` attribute — CSS-only, visible on hover and `:focus-visible`. Alert: `.alert` with `.mod-info`, `.mod-success`, `.mod-warning`, `.mod-danger` modifiers using left-border colour coding. Modal: `.modal-backdrop`, `.modal`, `.modal-close`, `.modal-title`, `.modal-body` — ARIA usage (`role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, `aria-labelledby`) and keyboard requirements (Tab focus trap, Escape close, backdrop click close) documented in comments. `.is-hidden` toggles display. **controls.css** — Page-specific stylesheet for clock-controls.html. Fixed dark terminal theme using `--ctrl-*` custom property namespace — not system-preference responsive. Defines: `.panel`, `.panel-nav`, `.panel-nav-link`, `.panel-nav-id`, `.panel-title`, `.section-title`, `.ctrl-section`, `.ctrl-radio-label` (with `.is-active` state), `.ctrl-field`, `.ctrl-field-label`, text and number input overrides, `.ctrl-btn` (hover and active states), `.ctrl-note`, `.ctrl-status`. State class `.is-hidden` hides inactive sections. `prefers-reduced-motion` block disables transitions. **utilities.css** — Single-purpose helper classes with `u-` prefix. Display utilities: `.u-flex`, `.u-flex-center`, `.u-flex-col`, `.u-block`, `.u-inline`, `.u-inline-flex`, `.u-hidden`. Spacing utilities: `.u-m-0`, `.u-p-0`, `.u-mt-auto`, `.u-ml-auto`, `.u-mr-auto`. Text utilities: `.u-text-center`, `.u-text-left`, `.u-text-right`, `.u-text-muted`, `.u-text-sm`, `.u-text-xs`. State classes: `.is-hidden`, `.is-visible`. Screen-reader utility: `.u-sr-only` (visually hidden via `clip` and 1px dimensions) with `:focus-visible` reveal for skip-to-content links. **banner.css** — `.banner` container and `.banner-title` with `clamp()` fluid typography in `em`. Mobile-first breakpoints. `prefers-contrast` and `prefers-reduced-motion` blocks. **border-effect.css** — `.border-effect` component with animated conic-gradient border. `prefers-reduced-motion` disables the rotation animation. --- ## JavaScript **clock.js** — Kinetic grid engine. Reads configuration from localStorage via `loadConfig()` on initialisation and on `storage` events. Computes hand angles for clock mode (HH:MM with UTC offset) and countdown mode (MM:SS with zero state). Pre-allocated angle buffers (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) eliminate per-frame heap allocations. `Float64Array` angle cache in `apply()` skips DOM writes for unchanged values. Pattern trig phase throttled to ~30fps; ease phases run at 60fps; static display at 1fps. rAF loop pauses via `visibilitychange`. **favicon-animator.js** — IIFE module (`FaviconAnimator`). Initialises on `DOMContentLoaded`. Creates an off-screen 32×32 canvas with a linear gradient stroke. rAF loop throttled to ~10fps draws a progressive square outline edge-by-edge each cycle, then writes the result to `favicon.href` via `canvas.toDataURL()`. Pauses when tab is hidden; resumes on visibility restore. **logger.js** — Development logger. Buffered in memory (`_buf` array) — no storage I/O on `log()` calls. Flushes to `sessionStorage` (`ap_dev_log`) on `beforeunload`; writes a persistent summary to `localStorage` (`ap_dev_log_summary`). Self-tests localStorage and sessionStorage availability on init. Public API: `log(section, message, data)`, `time(name)`, `timeEnd(name)`, `flush()`, `dump()`, `clear()`. **main.js** — Reserved entry point. Not loaded by any current page. --- ## Assets **assets/icons/** — SVG icons: `bluesky-icon.svg`, `claude-ai-icon.svg`, `soundcloud.svg`. **assets/audio/, assets/bgs/, assets/components/, assets/graphics/, assets/photos/** — Directory structure scaffolded for future use; no files currently placed. **Images** — Where used, `` elements include `alt`, `width`, and `height` attributes. `srcset` provides 250w and 500w resolution variants; `sizes` attribute reflects layout breakpoints. `loading="lazy"` on below-fold images; `loading="eager"` on the first visible image. --- ## File Relationships ``` index.html ├── styles/clock.css Clock tokens, grid, hand styles, keyframes ├── js/favicon-animator.js Animated canvas favicon (defer) └── js/clock.js Clock engine — reads localStorage (defer) clock-controls.html ├── styles/controls.css Fixed dark panel styles ├── js/favicon-animator.js Animated canvas favicon (defer) └── [inline script] Form state, localStorage writes └── localStorage Read by clock.js on load + storage event Additional pages (non-clock) ├── styles/colors.css Design tokens ├── styles/fonts.css Font stacks ├── styles/global.css Base elements + 12-col grid ├── styles/components.css UI components ├── styles/[page].css Page-specific overrides └── styles/utilities.css Helper classes ```

Initialises on `DOMContentLoaded`. Creates an off-screen 32×32 canvas with a linear gradient stroke. rAF loop throttled to ~10fps draws a progressive square outline edge-by-edge each cycle, then writes the result to `favicon.href` via `canvas.toDataURL()`. Pauses when tab is hidden; resumes on visibility restore. **logger.js** — Development logger. Buffered in memory (`_buf` array) — no storage I/O on `log()` calls. Flushes to `sessionStorage` (`ap_dev_log`) on `beforeunload`; writes a persistent summary to `localStorage` (`ap_dev_log_summary`). Self-tests localStorage and sessionStorage availability on init. Public API: `log(section, message, data)`, `time(name)`, `timeEnd(name)`, `flush()`, `dump()`, `clear()`. **main.js** — Shared application utilities, loaded on all pages with `defer`. Sets `--vw` and `--vh` CSS custom properties from `window.innerWidth`/`Height` on load and on `resize` (rAF-throttled), fixing iOS Safari's failure to re-evaluate `dvh`/`dvw` units after orientation change. --- ## Assets **assets/icons/** — SVG icons: `bluesky-icon.svg`, `claude-ai-icon.svg`, `soundcloud.svg`. **assets/audio/, assets/bgs/, assets/components/, assets/graphics/, assets/photos/** — Directory structure scaffolded for future use; no files currently placed. **Images** — Where used, `` elements include `alt`, `width`, and `height` attributes. `srcset` provides 250w and 500w resolution variants; `sizes` attribute reflects layout breakpoints. `loading="lazy"` on below-fold images; `loading="eager"` on the first visible image. --- ## File Relationships ``` index.html ├── styles/clock.css Clock tokens, grid, hand styles, keyframes ├── js/favicon-animator.js Animated canvas favicon (defer) └── js/clock.js Clock engine — reads localStorage (defer) clock-controls.html ├── styles/controls.css Fixed dark panel styles ├── js/favicon-animator.js Animated canvas favicon (defer) └── [inline script] Form state, localStorage writes └── localStorage Read by clock.js on load + storage event Additional pages (non-clock) ├── styles/colors.css Design tokens ├── styles/fonts.css Font stacks ├── styles/global.css Base elements + 12-col grid ├── styles/components.css UI components ├── styles/[page].css Page-specific overrides └── styles/utilities.css Helper classes ```

## What the site is

Kinetic clock art piece. 84 analogue mini-clocks (6 rows × 14 cols) animate through generative patterns then resolve to display current time or a countdown. Three pages: index.html (clock), clock-controls.html (settings), play.html (SoundCloud playlists).

Stack: vanilla HTML + CSS + JS. No build tools, no frameworks, no external dependencies.

## CSS load order

### index.html
1. styles/colors.css — design tokens
2. styles/global.css — body flex centring, base resets
3. styles/clock.css — clock tokens, .grid-14x6, .mc, .hand, countdown-zero keyframes (body.clock scoped)
4. styles/utilities.css — u-* helpers
5. styles/home.css — --side-* tokens, aside, social links, Bluesky flutter, Claude/SoundCloud hover effects

### clock-controls.html
- styles/controls.css only (self-contained dark theme, --ctrl-* tokens)

### play.html
- styles/play.css only (self-contained, includes its own box-sizing reset)

## JS files

| File | Purpose | Loaded on |
|---|---|---|
| js/main.js | Shared utilities: rAF-throttled --vw/--vh viewport fix | All pages (defer) |
| js/clock.js | rAF loop, pattern engine, angle application | index.html (defer) |
| js/controls.js | Validated localStorage read/write, debounced inputs, section toggle | clock-controls.html (defer) |
| js/favicon-animator.js | Canvas favicon ~10fps, self-inits at load | index.html (defer) |
| js/logger.js | Dev logger — opt-in, not loaded by default | — |

## Key constraints (must not be violated)

- `.grid-14x6` class on the grid div; `id="grid"` is the JS hook — never remove the id
- `body.clock` on index.html body — clock-specific body overrides are scoped to this class
- No inline styles anywhere
- No max-width media queries — mobile-first min-width only
- No :visited state
- font-size: em units; spacing/layout: rem units
- All colours via CSS custom properties — no bare hex in component CSS
- clock.css and clock.js: do not change visual behaviour, timing, or pattern maths
- No !important anywhere (except prefers-reduced-motion block in global.css which is acceptable)

## Orientation change fix (added 2026-03-20)

iOS Safari doesn't reliably re-evaluate dvh/dvw CSS units after orientation change. Fix:
- main.js sets --vw and --vh CSS custom properties on document.documentElement from window.innerWidth/Height
- resize listener is rAF-throttled (rafPending flag)
- clock.css uses calc(var(--vw, 1dvw) * N) and calc(var(--vh, 1dvh) * N) with dvw/dvh as no-JS fallbacks
- main.js loaded on both index.html and clock-controls.html

## Performance notes

- 168 .hand elements: will-change:transform promotes each to GPU compositor layer
- .mc: contain:layout style paint scopes recalc to each cell
- rAF loops pause on visibilitychange (both clock.js and favicon-animator.js)
- Pre-allocated angle buffers — zero per-frame heap allocations
- Float64Array cache skips DOM writes when angle unchanged
- Pattern trig ~30fps throttled; ease phases 60fps; static display 1fps

## CSS token namespaces

- --color-* : global design tokens (colors.css)
- --clk-* : clock page tokens (clock.css)
- --ctrl-* : controls page tokens (controls.css, isolated)
- --side-* : aside.side tokens (home.css, isolated)
- --banner-* : banner component tokens (banner.css)

## Social icons (index.html)

- Bluesky: inline SVG butterfly (class=bsky-flutter), flutter animation in home.css
- SoundCloud: assets/icons/soundcloud.svg, class=links-soundcloud, ripple animation in home.css
- Claude credit: assets/icons/claude-ai-icon.svg, class=credit-claude, spin animation in home.css

## Loader

- `.loader` div with mini clock face rendered before rAF starts
- Dismissed by clock.js using a double-rAF after first paint

## Favicon animator

- `init()` removes all static favicon `<link>` elements and injects a fresh canvas-backed one on startup
- Self-inits at bottom of favicon-animator.js — do NOT call FaviconAnimator.init() from main.js

## Known remaining issues (iteration 2)

- WCAG contrast: `.side p` text #727786 on #292b31 = ~2.85:1, fails AA 4.5:1
- home.css aside positioning: right:calc(-50vw + 1.25rem) and top:-17rem are magic-number values, fragile
- home.css .side p uses Gill Sans — deviates from project's two-font system (Verdana/Arial)

## Git state

Two clean commits. Here's the full summary:

---

## Iteration 1 — Done ✅

**7 fixes applied across 8 files:**

| Fix | File | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `var` → `const/let`, rAF throttle, DOMContentLoaded removed | `main.js` | Convention + perf |
| Added to `clock-controls.html` | `main.js` | Consistency — viewport fix applies on controls page too |
| Debounce (250ms) + localStorage sanitization/range clamping | `controls.js` | Security + perf |
| Hardcoded hex → `--side-*` CSS custom properties; double-semicolon typo fixed | `home.css` | Convention |
| `#ffffff` → `--banner-title-color` custom property | `banner.css` | Convention |
| Explanatory comment for `--vw`/`--vh` approach | `clock.css` | Clarity |
| Deleted | `critical.css` | Dead file |

---

## Remaining Issues (Iteration 2)

**Raise — accessibility (design impact, not auto-fixed):**
- `.side p` text `#727786` on `#292b31` background = **~2.85:1** contrast ratio. Fails WCAG AA (4.5:1 required). Lightening to `#909aac` or similar would fix it without dramatically changing appearance.

**Raise — CSS fragility:**
- `home.css` `aside.side` uses `right: calc(-50vw + 1.25rem)` and `top: -17rem` — magic numbers that depend on the viewport width matching assumptions. Likely to break if the aside content changes or at unusual viewport widths. `writing-mode: vertical-rl` would be a more robust approach.

**Raise — typography convention:**
- `home.css` `.side p` uses `font-family: 'Gill Sans', 'Gill Sans MT', Calibri, 'Trebuchet MS', sans-serif` — deviates from the project's declared two-font system (Verdana headings / Arial body). Fine as an intentional design choice, but worth noting for consistency.

**Not an issue (decisions confirmed):**
- `will-change: transform` on 168 `.hand` elements — deliberate, CLAUDE.md-documented GPU compositor optimisation
- `controls.css` `--ctrl-*` tokens — intentionally isolated, not in colors.css
- `play.css` box-sizing reset — needed, play.html has no global.css
- `max-height: 500px` in orientation query — `max-height` is valid; only `max-width` is banned

Labels, inputs, and buttons are large and readable. - **Clean and minimal.** No decoration that doesn't serve the experience. - **Orientation aware.** The clock grid adapts gracefully to landscape on small-screen devices. - **Reduced motion respect.** The controls page respects `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. - **Keyboard accessible.** Every interactive element reachable by Tab, operable by Enter/Space, dismissable by Escape. --- ## SEO Features - Descriptive `` on every page. - `<meta name="description">` on every page. - Open Graph `og:` tags — title, description, type, image, url. - `<link rel="canonical">` on every page. - Schema.org JSON-LD structured data (`WebSite`, `WebApplication`). - Semantic HTML5 landmarks: `<main>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<footer>` where appropriate. - `lang="en"` on `<html>`. - `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` on indexable pages. - `site.webmanifest` for PWA and search discoverability. --- ## Performance Features - Zero third-party scripts or stylesheets on `index.html`. - Critical CSS (`clock.css`) loaded as a single `<link>` in `<head>`. - System fonts only — zero font-loading requests on the main clock page. - All `<img>` elements have `width`, `height`, `alt`; below-fold images use `loading="lazy"`. - `srcset` with `250w` and `500w` resolution variants; `sizes` reflects layout breakpoints. - Explicit image dimensions prevent Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS). - `defer` attribute on all `<script>` tags. - `requestAnimationFrame` for the clock render loop — no layout-triggering properties animated. - CSS animations use `transform` and `opacity` only. - `aspect-ratio` on the clock grid prevents CLS on load. - `will-change: transform` on `.hand` — promotes all 168 hand elements to GPU compositor layers; JS-driven `rotate()` updates are applied by the compositor thread, not the main thread. - `contain: layout style paint` on `.mc` — scopes style recalculation to each clock cell; a transform write on one hand cannot cascade across the document. - Pre-allocated angle buffers in `clock.js` (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) — eliminates per-frame heap allocations. - `Float64Array` angle cache in `apply()` — skips DOM write if the value hasn't changed. - Pattern phase throttled to ~30fps; ease-in/out windows at 60fps; static display at 1fps.
